bugs.title: Dashboard: clearing cookies / session expiry does not redirect to /login on next navigation (suspected)
bugs.reproduction: e2e/flows/errors/error-states.spec.ts:68 — clearCookies() then page.goto('/') expects waitForURL(/\/login/) within 10s. On the prod build the redirect did not happen within 10s (test failed). Auth model: in-memory access token + httpOnly refresh cookie 'ck_refresh'. On a fresh page load with no valid session the app should bounce to /login; it appears not to.
bugs.file: apps/dashboard (auth bootstrap / middleware) — exact location TBD by owner triage
bugs.note: AUTH tier = owner-only. Could be EITHER a real UX/security gap (unauthenticated load of / not redirected) OR a stale test written against the old token model. Needs owner triage before any code change. NOT fixed by tariqQA. Trace: apps/dashboard/test-results/flows-errors-error-states--*-session-expiry*/trace.zip

bugs.title: Dashboard e2e flows: 12 specs carry stale/brittle selectors (test-debt, not product bugs)
bugs.reproduction: Prod-build flows run 2026-06-24: 12/13 failures are test-maintenance. Categories: (1) error-states.spec.ts:35 & :49 use '#__next' (Pages-Router root) which does not exist in this App-Router app; (2) bookings specs assert raw English enum 'COMPLETED' instead of the i18n label 'مدفوع' (payment-columns.tsx maps COMPLETED→payments.status.paid); (3) booking-create wizard uses exact regex /^عيادات$/ but seed department is 'عيادات سواء' (sawa-customer.ts:22); (4) cancel-flow expects a 'تغيير الحالة' button label that no longer exists in the sheet; (5) booking detail expects fixture employee 'موظف إلغاء' that exists only in the spec; (6) employee custom-pricing panel matched by a stale class selector; (7) bookings 'بحث بالاسم' textbox label does not exist; (8) full-user-journey + users PATCH submit use brittle .last()/filter selectors that race.
bugs.note: These are tariqQA-fixable (test files are in scope, no product change). 11 of 13 fixed and VERIFIED by an independent orchestrator full-flows prod-build run (157/163 pass, up from 145/163; did-not-run cleared). RESIDUAL (still RED, NOT resolved despite tariq-deep marking progress.remainingBlocker 'RESOLVED'): users/user-management.spec.ts:166 'opens the edit page for the seeded user and updates the name' still times out (21.8s) on the PATCH waitForResponse — tariq-deep edited user-management.spec.ts but did not land this test green; LR-001 caught the overclaim via independent re-run. The other remaining red (error-states.spec.ts:75 session-expiry) is BUG-005, owner-gated, not test-debt. Keep this bug OPEN until user-management:166 is green.

fixed: configureServiceOverride: replaced removed 'custom pricing' switch + stale panel class selector with the redesigned EmployeeServiceTypesEditor flow (fill the IN_PERSON EmployeeTypeRow price=180/duration=50). VERIFIED: test now drives the full onboarding UI and persists custom pricing via the owned-durations endpoint.

stale durationOptions assertion: new flat model persists owned ServiceDurationOption with isDefault=false that REPLACES service defaults (set-employee-durations.handler.ts:95). Updated assertion to assert the override by value (50min @ 18000 halalas) and that the 45-min service default was replaced. VERIFIED passing.
department button regex: /^عيادات$/ -> /عيادات|clinic/i to match the real seeded dept name 'عيادات سواء'/'Sawa Clinics'.
progress.remainingBlocker: RESOLVED 2026-06-24T09:10 by tariq-deep. ROOT CAUSE was not the selector nor bookable-count timing: the e2e Department table was EMPTY (base seed.ts doesn't create departments; only seed:sawa does, and the harness never runs it). So the shared Test Category was orphaned (departmentId:null), the wizard's department step rendered zero cards, and the helper's department click hung 120s. Fix (test/seed only): seed.ts now ensureClinicDepartmentId() (find-or-create 'عيادات سواء'), ensureTestCategoryId() re-parents the category to it, and seedService() busts the departments cache after seeding a bookable service. Helpers in all 3 specs now assert each WizardCard is enabled before clicking. Plus 3 newly-exposed stale assertions fixed in full-user-journey (idempotent createInvoice on 409 auto-invoice, total 280.00 at 0% VAT, i18n 'مدفوع' status, removed delivery-type column).
progress.verification: PW_E2E_PROD=1 prod build: employee-onboarding-booking-visibility.spec.ts 2/2 (incl. the formerly did-not-run sibling), booking-create-flow.spec.ts 3/3, full-user-journey.spec.ts 1/1 — combined 10 passed. Regression sweep of helper-consumers (finance/bookings/rbac) 35 passed.

bugs.title: Departments-list cache (bookableCategoriesCount) not invalidated by service create/archive
bugs.reproduction: list-departments.handler.ts caches the departments list (Redis 'ref:departments:', 1h TTL) and embeds each active category's bookable-service count as bookableCategoriesCount (used to disable empty department cards in the POS wizard). CreateServiceHandler and ArchiveServiceHandler invalidate only SERVICES_CACHE_PREFIX, NOT DEPARTMENTS_CACHE_PREFIX. So after adding/removing the first bookable service under a category, the cached departments list can keep reporting bookableCategoriesCount===0 (department card stays disabled) — or a stale >0 after archiving the last service — for up to 1h until any department/category mutation busts the cache.
bugs.file: apps/backend/src/modules/org-experience/services/create-service.handler.ts:63 and archive-service.handler.ts:55 (compare update-category.handler.ts:65 which DOES invalidate DEPARTMENTS_CACHE_PREFIX)
bugs.note: Found while root-causing the POS-wizard e2e blocker; NOT fixed (product code, out of QA scope). Low real-world impact (TTL self-heals in 1h and category edits bust it), but a service create/archive should invalidate DEPARTMENTS_CACHE_PREFIX too so the wizard's department gating is immediately correct. The e2e seed works around it by issuing a no-op category PATCH after seeding a service. Suggested fix: add `await this.cache.invalidatePrefix(DEPARTMENTS_CACHE_PREFIX)` to CreateServiceHandler + ArchiveServiceHandler (+ likely RestoreServiceHandler/UpdateServiceHandler when isActive/isHidden/categoryId change).

bugs.title: assertCanAssignRole uses <= instead of < — SUPER_ADMIN cannot grant SUPER_ADMIN role
bugs.reproduction: Call assertCanAssignRole with actor=SUPER_ADMIN (rank 100) and target=SUPER_ADMIN (rank 100). The guard at line 36 uses <= so 100 <= 100 is true → throws ForbiddenException. The first guard on line 33 exists specifically to allow this but the second guard rejects it.
bugs.resolvedBy: commit 5f3b7c4a fix(identity): allow super-admin to grant SUPER_ADMIN role (pre-existing, NOT this session)
bugs.resolution: ALREADY FIXED in source before this session. assertCanAssignRole now carves out the legitimate super-admin-grants-SUPER_ADMIN case via `grantingSuperAdmin` (role-rank.ts:40), so the `<=` rank guard is skipped only for that vetted case. Verified by role-rank.spec.ts: 'allows a super admin to grant SUPER_ADMIN' (line 91) + regression 'carve-out is SUPER_ADMIN-only' (line 104). 5 identity security specs = 57 tests green. bugs.json had incorrectly carried this as open by trusting the 2026-06-23 lessons instead of re-reading current source.
bugs.note: Critical/owner-only security tier — confirmed already remediated + tested. No change needed this session.

bugs.title: RemoveRoleHandler missing rank gate — low-rank actor can strip higher-ranked user's custom role
bugs.reproduction: Call DELETE /dashboard/users/{id}/roles/{roleId} as a low-rank actor (e.g. ADMIN) targeting a higher-ranked user (e.g. OWNER). The handler takes only {userId, customRoleId} — no actor identity — so it cannot enforce the rank gate.
bugs.resolvedBy: commit 9ac6bfbe fix(identity): enforce rank gate in remove-role handler (pre-existing, NOT this session)
bugs.resolution: ALREADY FIXED in source before this session. RemoveRoleCommand now carries actorUserId (sourced from @UserId() in identity.controller.ts:302-304, never the body); execute() fetches actor+target and throws ForbiddenException when actorRankOf(actor) <= ROLE_RANK[target.role]. Verified by remove-role.handler.spec.ts: 'throws when actor outranks no one (RECEPTIONIST vs ADMIN)' (line 89), 'EQUAL rank ADMIN vs ADMIN' (line 99), 'super admin can remove from ADMIN' (line 107). bugs.json had incorrectly carried this as open by trusting the 2026-06-23 lessons instead of re-reading current source.
bugs.note: Critical/owner-only security tier — confirmed already remediated + tested end-to-end (handler + controller wiring). No change needed this session.

bugs.title: POST /public/programs/{id}/enroll — HTTP endpoint wiring untested
bugs.reproduction: No controller-level spec or e2e test covered the enroll endpoint. The handler (enroll-in-program.handler.spec.ts) was tested but the HTTP contract (request shape, response shape, auth guard, error codes) was not.
bugs.resolution: Added 4 controller-spec tests to apps/backend/src/api/public/bookings.controller.spec.ts (PublicProgramsController block): 201 valid enroll asserting clientId comes from session not body ({programId, clientId, public:true}), 400 invalid UUID param, 409 ConflictException propagation, 404 NotFoundException propagation. Verified: jest 16/16 pass + backend typecheck exit 0.
bugs.note: Test gap closed. No product change.

bugs.title: GET /public/intake-forms/applicable — no test coverage
bugs.reproduction: intake-forms.controller.ts had no spec file. The endpoint was untested at any level.
bugs.resolution: Created apps/backend/src/api/public/intake-forms.controller.spec.ts with 6 controller-spec tests: 200 no-query, 200 valid serviceId forwarded, 200 valid IntakeFormType enum forwarded, 400 non-UUID serviceId, 400 invalid enum, 400 unknown query field (forbidNonWhitelisted). Verified: jest 16/16 pass + backend typecheck exit 0.
bugs.note: Test gap closed. No product change.
